commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an optional heartbeat `hint`, threads it through skipped heartbeat events, and renders that hint in `openclaw status --deep` while preserving `reason: "target-none"`.

PR surface: Source +20, Tests +55. Total +75 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source shows the `target=none` resolver emits `reason: "target-none"` without a hint, and the status formatter falls back to `unknown` when the skipped event has no channel.
commands.review_followup: - The branch already includes the narrow code change, regression tests, and refreshed real behavior proof; remaining work is maintainer review and normal merge gating.

target.title: fix(heartbeat): rename target-none skip reason to delivery-disabled
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: The default heartbeat target is `none`, which is documented to mean "the heartbeat runs but does not deliver externally". When that path is taken, the skipped heartbeat event reports `reason: "target-none"`, which operators consistently misread as "no recipient/session was resolved" and start debugging channels, presence, allowlists, and session routing â€” none of which are involved. Issue #44534 documents the misread.
- **Why it matters**: misleading diagnostic wording costs operator time during incident triage and obscures correct, by-design behavior.
- **What changed (additive, no string rename)**: rather than rename the machine reason (which would break operators who parse `target-none`), this adds an optional human-readable `hint`. `reason` stays `"target-none"`; a new `hint: "delivery disabled by configuration"` is populated for the `target=none` skip and surfaced in the `openclaw status` Last-heartbeat row. A follow-up commit also drops the misleading `unknown` channel placeholder for skipped rows (see Root Cause / P3).
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: no change to the `reason` string contract, heartbeat scheduling, delivery resolution, target priority, or defaults. The `hint` is optional everywhere and clients fall back to `reason` when absent.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes #44534
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: the `target=none` skip now carries an additive `hint` ("delivery disabled by configuration") while keeping `reason: "target-none"`, and the `openclaw status` Last-heartbeat row surfaces that hint without the misleading `unknown` channel placeholder.
- **Real environment tested**: local source checkout on Windows 11 (Node 24.15.0, pnpm 11.2.2) at the patched HEAD. Ran a Node + tsx script that imports `resolveHeartbeatDeliveryTarget` and `buildStatusLastHeartbeatValue` from the patched source and prints both the resolver output and the rendered status row.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: from repo root, ran `node --import tsx ./proof.mjs` where `proof.mjs` imports `resolveHeartbeatDeliveryTarget` from `src/infra/outbound/targets.ts` and `buildStatusLastHeartbeatValue` from `src/commands/status.command-sections.ts`, then prints the resolver result for `{}` and `{ agents: { defaults: { heartbeat: { target: "none" } } } }`, plus the status row for a config-disabled skip, a hintless skip, a channel-less sent heartbeat, and a sent heartbeat with a channel.
- **Evidence after fix**: live stdout copied from `node --import tsx ./proof.mjs`; no model invocation involved.
  ```text
  ## resolveHeartbeatDeliveryTarget (target unset / none)
  # default (unset target)
  {
    "channel": "none",
    "reason": "target-none",
    "hint": "delivery disabled by configuration"
  }

  # explicit target=none
  {
    "channel": "none",
    "reason": "target-none",
    "hint": "delivery disabled by configuration"
  }

  ## openclaw status â€” Last heartbeat row (buildStatusLastHeartbeatValue)
  # config-disabled skip (target-none + hint)
  skipped Â· 2m ago Â· delivery disabled by configuration

  # skip without hint (e.g. no-target)
  skipped Â· 2m ago

  # sent heartbeat, no channel
  sent Â· 2m ago Â· unknown

  # sent heartbeat with channel
  sent Â· 2m ago Â· telegram
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: `reason` stays `target-none` (string contract unchanged); the additive `hint` is populated and reaches the status row, which now reads `skipped Â· 2m ago Â· delivery disabled by configuration` instead of the previous `skipped Â· 2m ago Â· unknown Â· â€¦`. A sent heartbeat with no channel still shows `unknown`, and a resolved channel is preserved.
- **What was not tested**: full live gateway service startup + `openclaw status --deep` against an installed gateway with an enabled channel. Not required â€” `resolveHeartbeatDeliveryTarget` is the single emission site for the reason/hint, `runHeartbeatOnce` passes `delivery.hint` straight into the skipped heartbeat event, and `buildStatusLastHeartbeatValue` is the single status formatter; all three are covered by unit tests and the proof above.
- **Before evidence**: documented in the issue body of #44534 (operator hit the misleading `target-none` reason in the wild on 2026.4.x).

## Root Cause

- **Root cause**: the skip reason string is named after the implementation detail (`target` value is `none`) rather than the operator-facing meaning. An earlier revision of this PR renamed the reason to `delivery-disabled`, but review flagged the compatibility risk for anyone parsing the machine reason. This revision keeps the reason and adds a human-readable companion instead.
- **P3 follow-up (status row)**: `buildStatusLastHeartbeatValue` rendered `channel ?? "unknown"`. A skipped heartbeat has no delivery channel by design, so the row read `skipped Â· â€¦ Â· unknown Â· delivery disabled by configuration` â€” the `unknown` reads as a resolution failure. The fix drops the `unknown` placeholder for skipped heartbeats (the hint carries the explanation); non-skipped heartbeats without a channel still show `unknown`, and a resolved channel is always shown.
- **Missing detection / guardrail**: there were no focused `buildStatusLastHeartbeatValue` tests. This PR adds them, plus the resolver test cases assert `reason: "target-none"` + the `hint`.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file**: `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts` (resolver returns `reason: "target-none"` + `hint` for the disabled-delivery branches); `src/commands/status.command-sections.test.ts` (new `buildStatusLastHeartbeatValue` cases: config-disabled skip, hintless skip, channel-less sent, sent-with-channel).
- **Scenario the test should lock in**: the disabled-delivery skip keeps the stable `target-none` reason and gains a hint; the status row shows the hint and never prints `unknown` for a skip.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail**: the resolver is the single source of the reason/hint and the formatter is the single status renderer, so unit coverage on both pins the behavior end to end.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any)**: the resolver test cases existed and were updated from the earlier `delivery-disabled` revision to assert `target-none` + hint.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw status --deep` Last-heartbeat row for a config-disabled skip now reads `skipped Â· <age> ago Â· delivery disabled by configuration` instead of `skipped Â· <age> ago Â· unknown`. The machine `reason` value is unchanged (`target-none`). No other behavior changes.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment
- OS: Windows 11
- Runtime: Node 24.15.0, pnpm 11.2.2
- Model/provider: N/A (heartbeat delivery target resolution + status formatting; no model invoked)
- Integration/channel: heartbeat default config (target unset â†’ resolves to `none`)
- Relevant config (redacted): synthetic in-memory config snapshots; synthetic `HeartbeatEventPayload` values for the status formatter

### Steps
1. Apply this patch.
2. From repo root, run a Node + tsx script importing `resolveHeartbeatDeliveryTarget` and `buildStatusLastHeartbeatValue` from the patched source.
3. Print the resolver result for unset and explicit `target: "none"`, and the status row for skipped/sent payloads.

### Expected
- Resolver: `reason: "target-none"`, `hint: "delivery disabled by configuration"`.
- Status row (skip): `skipped Â· 2m ago Â· delivery disabled by configuration` (no `unknown`).

### Actual
- Matches expected (see Evidence above).

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after â€” the new `buildStatusLastHeartbeatValue` cases fail against the old `channel ?? "unknown"` formatter; they pass on this patch.
- [x] Trace/log snippets â€” see Real behavior proof above.
- [ ] Screenshot/recording â€” N/A (text/log change).
- [ ] Perf numbers â€” N/A.

Targeted Vitest runs after the patch:

```text
$ pnpm test src/commands/status.command-sections.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  51 passed (51)
```

Formatting + whitespace:

```text
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/status.command-sections.ts src/commands/status.command-sections.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

$ git diff --check
# clean
```

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: resolver returns `target-none` + hint for unset and explicit `target: "none"`; status row renders the hint without `unknown` for a skip, shows `unknown` for a channel-less sent heartbeat, and preserves a resolved channel. Ran the two targeted Vitest files (51 tests) and the proof script.
- **Edge cases checked**: hintless skip (`no-target`) renders `skipped Â· 2m ago` with no dangling separator; sent-with-channel unaffected.
- **What I did NOT verify**: full live gateway `openclaw status --deep` against an installed gateway; the formatter + resolver unit coverage pins the rendered output.

## Review Conversations
- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes. `reason` keeps its `target-none` value; the `hint` is additive and optional. No external consumer of `reason` is affected.
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Failure Recovery

- How to disable/revert: revert the commits on this branch. The `hint` field and the status-row tweak are removed; `reason` was never changed.

## Risks and Mitigations

The behavior change is limited to additive diagnostic output (a new optional `hint`) and the removal of a misleading `unknown` channel token for skipped status rows. No machine-readable contract changes.

## AI-assisted

This PR was prepared with AI assistance. I reviewed the diff, ran the targeted Vitest lanes, oxfmt check, and the real-behavior proof locally, and confirm the output above is from my machine.
